name: Oven-Baked Bourguignonne
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Medium Or Broad Egg Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut bonless chuck into 1-inch cubes as with stew meat.
2. Peel and chop tomatoes.
3. Choose either onion or beefy onion soup mix.
4. Use 1 cup of sliced mushrooms or slice up 8 oz fresh mushrooms.
5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
6. Toss beef with flour, then bake uncovered for 20 minutes in a 2-quart casserole.
7. Add carrots, tomatoes, and bay leaf. Add beefy onion soup mix blended with wine. Bake, covered, for 1 1/2 hours or until beef is tender.
8. Add mushrooms and bake covered for an additional 10 minutes. Remove the bay leaf.
9. Cook noodles according to package directions.
10. Serve bourguignonne over noodles.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
1. Toss beef with flour, set aside.
2. In a 2-quart casserole, combine tomatoes, bay leaf, and beefy onion soup mix blended with wine. Heat, covered, at HIGH (Full Power) for 7 minutes, stirring once.
3. Add beef and carrots. Heat, covered, at DEFROST (30% FULL POWER), stirring occasionally, for 1 1/4 hours.
4. Add mushrooms. Heat, covered, at DEFROST (30% FULL POWER), for 30 minutes or until beef is tender. Remove the bay leaf. Let stand covered for 5 minutes.
FREEZING/REHEATING DIRECTIONS:
1. Bake bourguignonne and then freeze in a covered casserole.
2. Wrap the casserole tightly with heavy-duty aluminum foil.
3. To reheat, unwrap and bake at 400 degrees F (200 degrees C) for 1 hour, stirring occasionally to separate beef and vegetables. Alternatively, microwave on HIGH (Full Power), stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes or until heated through. Let stand covered for 5 minutes before serving.
